title: A call center technical specialist spends varying amount of time in each call to resolve the concern. Let’s say X follow…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: A call center technical specialist spends varying amount of time in each call to resolve the concern. Let’s say X follows Exponential distribution with a parameter of 0.2. Find P (x > 6)

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
We need to find the probability that the time taken by the technical specialist to resolve a call is greater than 6. 

Using the exponential distribution, the probability density function is given by:

f(x) = λe^(-λx)

where λ is the parameter of the distribution. In this case, λ = 0.2.

The cumulative distribution function for the exponential distribution is given by:

F(x) = 1 - e^(-λx)

We can now calculate the probability as:

P(x > 6) = 1 - P(x ≤ 6)
           = 1 - F(6)
           = 1 - (1 - e^(-0.2 × 6))
           = e^(-1.2)
           ≈ 0.301

Therefore, the probability that the time taken by the technical specialist to resolve a call is greater than 6 is approximately 0.301.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: A call center technical specialist spends varying amount of time in each call to resolve the concern. Let’s say X follows Exponential distribution with a parameter of 0.2. Find the median

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The median of an exponential distribution with a parameter λ is given by:

median = ln(2)/λ

In this case, the parameter λ = 0.2. 

Substituting the values, we get:

median = ln(2)/0.2
          ≈ 3.47

Therefore, the median time taken by the technical specialist to resolve a call is approximately 3.47 units of time.
